title: vulpia bromoides subsp. broteroi
intro: Vulpia bromoides subsp. broteroi, also known as the broom fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Portugal, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. Vulpia bromoides subsp. broteroi can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in tinctures and topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
